The Jews were very proud of their temple. But what satisfaction can the Eternal Mind take in a building made by human hands? God has a heaven and an earth of his own making, and temples built by people; but he overlooks them, so that he may look with favor to someone who is humble in spirit and serious, self-abasing and self-denying; whose heart truly sorrows for sin: such a heart is a living temple for God. The sacrifice of the wicked is not only unacceptable, but an offense to God. And whoever now offers a sacrifice according to the law, does in effect set aside Christ's sacrifice. Whoever burns incense, shows contempt for the incense of Christ’s intercession, and is as if he blessed an idol. People will be deceived by the empty confidences with which they deceive themselves. Unbelieving hearts, and unpurified consciences, need no more to make them miserable than to have their own fears brought upon them. Whatever people put in place of the priesthood, atonement, and intercession of Christ, will be found hateful to God.

The prophet turns to those who trembled at God’s word, to comfort and encourage them. The Lord will appear, to the joy of the humble believer, and to the confusion of hypocrites and persecutors. When the Spirit was poured out, and the gospel went forth from Zion, many people were converted in a short time. The word of God, especially his promises and ordinances, are consolations for the church. The true happiness of all Christians is increased by every person brought to Christ. The gospel brings with it, wherever it is received in its power, such a river of peace as will carry us to an ocean of boundless and endless bliss. Divine comforts reach the inner person; the joy of the Lord will be the strength of the believer. Both God’s mercy and justice shall be manifested, and forever magnified.

A prophetic declaration is given of the Lord's vengeance on all enemies of his church, especially that of all anti-Christian opposers of the gospel in the latter days, sets forth the abundance of means for the conversion of sinners. These expressions are figurative, and express the plentiful and gracious helps for bringing God’s elect home to Christ. All shall be welcome; and nothing will be lacking for their assistance and encouragement. A gospel ministry shall be set up in the church; they would have solemn worship before the Lord. In the last verse the nature of the punishment of sinners in the world to come is represented. Then the righteous and wicked will be separated. Our Savior applies this to the everlasting misery and torment of impenitent sinners in the future state. To the honor of that free grace which thus distinguishes them, let the redeemed of the Lord, with humility, and not without holy trembling, sing triumphant songs. With this affecting representation of the opposite states of the righteous and wicked, characters which include the whole human race, Isaiah concludes his prophecies. May God grant, for Christ’s sake, that our portion may be with those who fear and love his name, who cleave to his truths, and persevere in every good work, looking to receive from the Lord Jesus Christ the gracious invitation, Come, you blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.
